finding: A resolving HTTPS URL is insufficient deployment proof unless its authority is bound to the expected Cloudflare Pages project and the deployment URL is an immutable hash-labeled preview origin rather than only a mutable branch or production alias.

cases: immutable deployment plus production alias accepted
branch alias accepted only as alias
mutable branch alias rejected as deployment identity
credentials, port, path, query, and fragment rejected

observed: The existing deployment proof validator required HTTPS but did not constrain the Cloudflare Pages project hostname or require an immutable deployment origin.
Five deterministic tests passed against byte-identical module and test content before commit.
inferred: Without an authority policy, a valid HTTPS URL or mutable alias could be mistaken for immutable Cloudflare deployment identity.
proposed: Wire the policy into the deployment workflow immediately after Wrangler output capture and before served-identity verification.
unresolved: No successful Cloudflare deployment URL is available yet because repository environment credentials remain externally unconfigured.
The policy has not yet been exercised against a real Wrangler deployment response.

privacy_boundary: Stores only project hostname classes, normalized origins, reason codes, and commit metadata; never token values, account identifiers, headers, page content, health material, or private chat.
falsification_route: Run the policy against the next real Wrangler deployment-url and pages-deployment-alias-url outputs.
Reject the claim if a legitimate immutable deployment uses a hostname shape outside the declared hash.project.pages.dev contract.
Verify the accepted deployment origin serves the committed research-surface identity and source commit.
